Citations and Fines

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

Mutual de Seguridad – Accident Rate Certificate (Certificado de Siniestralidad) Sample Mutual doc
ISL Certificates of Occupational Risk Management – Chile

Please upload the Accident/Loss Rate Certificate (Certificado de Siniestralidad or Certificado de Accidentabilidad) issued by your company’s Administrator of the Work Accident and Occupational Disease Insurance (Seguro de la Ley 16.744)

If your company is affiliated with a Mutual (Mutual de Seguridad, ACHS, IST, etc.), please upload the Siniestralidad / Accident Rate Certificate (Certificado de Siniestralidad) issued by your Mutual insurer, including a list of injured persons (with names redacted).

For companies affiliated directly with the ISL, please upload your Accident Rate Certificate (Certificado de Accidentabilidad)
Please enter the Total Incident Rate for the given year.

If the Certificate of Accident/Loss Rate (Certificado de Tasa de Siniestralidad / Tasa de Cotización Adicional Diferenciada) issued by your Mutual or ISL does not include this specific data point, please enter “0” in this field.
Please enter the Severity Rate for the given year.

If the Certificate of Accident/Loss Rate (Certificado de Tasa de Siniestralidad / Tasa de Cotización Adicional Diferenciada) issued by your Mutual or ISL does not include this specific data point, please enter “0” in this field.
Please enter the Loss Work Day Rate for the given year.

If the Certificate of Accident/Loss Rate (Certificado de Tasa de Siniestralidad / Tasa de Cotización Adicional Diferenciada) issued by your Mutual or ISL does not include this specific data point, please enter “0” in this field.
Please enter the Frequency Rate for the given year.

The rate must have been issued within the last two years. This is the fee that employers and self-employed workers must pay associated with the risk of business activity. The additional contribution rate established in Supreme Decree No. 110 of 1968 of the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare must be paid by self-employed workers and by employers who begin their activities. The rate paid by employers may be reduced or recharged depending on their effective accident rate, which is measured in accordance with the provisions of Supreme Decree No. 67, of 1999, of the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare.
Please enter your company’s most recent differentiated additional contribution rate (Cotización Adicional Diferenciada).

Instances where the worker is left completely and permanently unable to perform any type of work, but does not require daily assistance.\n\nSUSESO – Pensión de Invalidez Permanente Total
Please enter the number of  Total Disability Accidents for the given year.
